MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE TRESPASS CAUSE OF ACTION. ALL ELEMENTS OF THIS CAUSE OF ACTION, INCLUDING DAMAGES ARE SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGED. DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AGNES KOZEL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances: Victor Marquez of the Marquez Law Group (415.848.8971) for defendant and moving party; No other appearances.

This matter is not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: SUSTAINED with leave to amend causes of action for interference with prescriptive easement, declaratory relief and fraud. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. Plaintiff shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint (1) reconciling allegations in the complaint and judicially noticeable documents; (2) alleging fraud with requisite particularity without relying on matters subject to Evid. Code 1152(a) and/or alleging facts showing that such matters are not subject to Evid. Code 1152(a) (e.g. SAC paragraph 18). Order signed. Judge: Anne Christine Massullo, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/ACM)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Ccp Section 438
Off Calendar Feb-16-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jocelyn S. Koo (415-981-5411) for CROSS DEFENDANT/Moving party MARION STARR.
Victor Marquez (415-314-7831) for Cross Complainant/opposing party Agnes Kosel.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Cross complainant submits to the tentative ruling but requests clarifications.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows with modifications:

CROSS DEFENDANT MARION STARR'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings CCP Section 438 is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to the trespass cause of action. All elements of this cause of action, including damages are sufficiently alleged. Motion is GRANTED as to all other causes of action subject to this motion for Cross-Complainant to allege each element of each cause of action and to plead fraud with specificity. Cross-Complainant shall also clarify whether she is seeking acknowledgment/imposition of easements and servitudes or she is seeking damages for breach of servitudes/easements as to the 1-3 causes of action. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
